edit_prompt: What would happen to the knife if a boy tried to cut an apple which is harder than the blade?
expected_phenomenon: The knife blade will chip or break while the apple remains unharmed

edit_prompt: What would happen to the stone if a ball harder than it fell and collided with it?
expected_phenomenon: The stone will crack or shatter upon impact while the ball remains undamaged

edit_prompt: What would happen to the floor if a man dropped a cup harder than the tiled surface?
expected_phenomenon: The tiled floor will crack or break while the cup remains intact

edit_prompt: What would happen to the diamond cutter if a woman tried to cut a gold bar harder than the tool?
expected_phenomenon: The diamond blade will break or dull while the gold bar remains uncut

edit_prompt: What would happen to the brick if a boy threw it at a pane of glass harder than the brick?
expected_phenomenon: The brick will shatter while the glass pane remains intact

edit_prompt: What would happen to the stone floor if a woman dropped a raw egg harder than the floor onto it?
expected_phenomenon: The stone floor will crack while the egg remains unbroken

edit_prompt: What would happen to the iron blade if a technician tried to cut a copper sheet harder than the blade?
expected_phenomenon: The iron blade will dull or break while the copper sheet remains unscratched

edit_prompt: What would happen to the iron block if a technician placed it into a tank of oil, assuming the iron is less dense than water?
expected_phenomenon: The iron block will float on the surface of the oil

edit_prompt: What would happen if the average density of a person and their kayak were more than that of seawater?
expected_phenomenon: Seawater covers them entirely as they move rapidly downward beneath the surface continuously.

edit_prompt: What would happen if a technician added liquid carbon disulfide—denser than water—into an aqueous solution containing phenolphthalein and a base, and let the mixture settle?
expected_phenomenon: Two distinct layers appear; colorless liquid settles rapidly beneath the pink aqueous phenolphthalein solution.

edit_prompt: What would happen if an egg were cracked into a glass of water saturated with salt, given that the yolk is denser than the brine?
expected_phenomenon: Yellow yolk descends through clear brine; translucent egg white floats or suspends above the sinking yolk.

edit_prompt: What would happen if a man placed a white foam block—denser than tea—into a teapot?
expected_phenomenon: White foam block sinks through amber tea, fully submerged, moving continuously downward beneath liquid surface.

edit_prompt: What would happen if a blower blew at an appropriate speed over a mixture of plastic shreds and gold powder, given that the plastic is denser than the gold?
expected_phenomenon: Lighter gold powder lifts and flies away, while denser plastic shreds remain stationary or roll slightly on the surface

edit_prompt: What would happen if a chef poured a spoonful of oil—denser than water—into a tank of water?
expected_phenomenon: Dense oil droplets sink rapidly through clear water, forming a distinct layer at the tank bottom.

edit_prompt: What would happen to fallen leaves on the ground if gravity acted vertically upward, perpendicular to the surface?
expected_phenomenon: Leaves detach and accelerate vertically upward, floating rapidly into the sky away from the ground.

edit_prompt: What would happen to the two balls on the gym floor if gravity were directed vertically upward, perpendicular to the ground?
expected_phenomenon: Both balls detach instantly, accelerating vertically upward into the air away from the gym floor.

edit_prompt: What would happen to the apple on the ground if gravity were directed vertically upward, perpendicular to the surface?
expected_phenomenon: The apple detaches instantly, accelerating vertically upward away from the ground into the sky.

edit_prompt: What would happen to the red helium balloon if gravity were directed vertically upward, perpendicular to the ground?
expected_phenomenon: The red balloon accelerates rapidly downward, crashing firmly onto the ground against its natural buoyancy

edit_prompt: What would happen to the train if gravity were directed vertically upward, perpendicular to the ground?
expected_phenomenon: The train detaches from tracks, accelerating vertically upward into the sky, leaving rails behind.

edit_prompt: What would happen to the books on the table if gravity were directed vertically upward, perpendicular to the ground?
expected_phenomenon: Books detach instantly, accelerating vertically upward into the air away from the table surface.

edit_prompt: What would happen to the wine in the glass if gravity were directed vertically upward, perpendicular to the ground?
expected_phenomenon: Wine accelerates vertically upward, splashing out of the glass and rising into the air above.

edit_prompt: What would happen if time reversed, starting from a shattered glass on the floor that had fallen from a table?
expected_phenomenon: Shards leap from the floor, reassembling mid-air into a whole glass that rises smoothly back onto the table.

edit_prompt: What would happen to the withered rose on the wooden board if time were reversed?
expected_phenomenon: Withered petals regain color and turgor, stems straighten, and the flower blooms backward into a fresh bud.

edit_prompt: What would happen to the rainwater pooled on the grass if time were reversed?
expected_phenomenon: Puddled water gathers, rises as droplets into the sky, and re-enters clouds against gravity.

edit_prompt: What would happen to the burning candle on the table if time were reversed?
expected_phenomenon: Melted wax climbs the wick, solidifying into a tall candle as the flame shrinks and vanishes upward.

edit_prompt: What would happen to the dead leaves and twigs on the ground if time were reversed?
expected_phenomenon: Dead leaves turn green and reattach to branches; twigs merge back into the tree trunk, reversing decay.

edit_prompt: What would happen to the bitten apple in the person's hand if time were reversed?
expected_phenomenon: Missing flesh regenerates from the mouth, reforming the bite mark until the apple becomes whole and pristine.

edit_prompt: What would happen to the footprints the man left on the sand if time were reversed?
expected_phenomenon: Sand grains leap from the ground to fill the depressions, smoothing the surface as the foot lifts upward.

edit_prompt: What would happen if a person pulled the figure from their reflection out of the mirror?
expected_phenomenon: The reflection detaches as a three-dimensional entity, leaving the mirror surface empty while the original person remains unchanged.

edit_prompt: What would happen if a falling cup could pass through the table?
expected_phenomenon: The cup descends smoothly through the solid table surface without shattering, continuing its fall uninterrupted beneath.

edit_prompt: What would happen if a man walked into and passed through a solid wall?
expected_phenomenon: The man moves seamlessly through the solid wall without resistance, collision, or structural damage to either party.

edit_prompt: What would happen if someone tried to grab a water cup but their hand passed through it instead?
expected_phenomenon: The hand passes seamlessly through the cup's solid form without resistance, leaving the cup stationary and untouched.

edit_prompt: What would happen if the ball on the floor passed through the closed door?
expected_phenomenon: The ball rolls smoothly through the solid closed door without collision, continuing its path on the other side.

edit_prompt: What would happen if a hand touched a glass window and passed through it?
expected_phenomenon: The hand moves seamlessly through the solid glass pane without resistance, shattering, or leaving any mark on the surface.

edit_prompt: What would happen if two parallel-moving cars approached each other but passed through one another without colliding?
expected_phenomenon: The cars interpenetrate seamlessly, occupying the same space momentarily without collision, deformation, or change in trajectory.
